name: Nu'man ibn Amr
title: Brother of Hallas
shortDescription: The brother of Hallas ibn Amr who came from Kufa to Karbala shoulder to shoulder with him and created a shared epic in defending Imam Hussain (AS).
fullStory:
Nu'man ibn Amr al-Rasibi was the brave and loyal brother of Hallas ibn Amr. Like his brother, he lived in Kufa and was considered a sincere Shia of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). The love for Wilayah was ingrained in their family.

Nu'man, along with his brother Hallas, by timely recognizing the danger and Imam Hussain's (AS) need for help, escaped from Kufa, which was under Ibn Ziyad's control. They faced many dangers on their way but reached Karbala relying on God.

On the day of Ashura, Nu'man and Hallas prepared for battle side by side. Their bond of brotherhood in the battlefield intertwined with their bond of faith, displaying beautiful scenes of selflessness and sacrifice.

In the all-out and cowardly attack of Umar Sa'd's army on the morning of Ashura, Nu'man drew his sword from its sheath and charged into the enemy army reciting war poetry. In coordination with his brother, he drove back a number of Kufan archers and soldiers.

Finally, after a long and tireless battle, Nu'man ibn Amr, like his brother, was targeted by arrows and sword strikes and attained the grace of martyrdom, so that his soul would be with his brother in Paradise as well.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى النُّعْمانِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو الرّاسِبِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Nu'man ibn Amr al-Rasibi.
